assembled: 2026-07-12 (Ensemble session 02; every VERIFIED figure checked first-hand by the conductor against the upstream instrument's committed record)
tier_note: VERIFIED = from the research wing's gauntleted instrument 013 'The Floor' (field-research/works/2026-07-09-the-floor/) and its claims ledger; IMAGINED = the game framing, marked. The engine must compute all outcomes from the numbers below at runtime — nothing about winning or losing may be hardcoded.

verified.subject: Google's data-center fleet, as disclosed in Google's own environmental reports
verified.floor_meaning: PUE = total facility energy / IT-equipment energy; IT energy is a subset of total, so the ratio cannot fall below 1.0 (The Green Grid; ISO/IEC 30134-2:2016).
verified.breakeven_formula: required_PUE = base_PUE / (1 + growth); base_PUE is the PRIOR year's fleet PUE (per-year base — 1.10 for the 2024 round, 1.09 for the 2025 round)
verified.winnable_condition: a round is winnable in principle iff required_PUE >= floor, i.e. growth_pct <= (base_PUE - 1.0) * 100 (10% at base 1.10; 9% at base 1.09)

rounds.growth_quote: compared to 17% growth in the prior year
rounds.growth_scope: total data center electricity consumption (2025 report's wording, describing 2023)

rounds.growth_quote: our total data center electricity consumption grew by 27% in 2024, compared to 17% growth in the prior year
rounds.growth_scope: total data center electricity consumption (2025 report, FY2024)

rounds.growth_quote: …our total electricity consumption grew by 37% in 2025, up from 27% in 2024
rounds.growth_scope: TOTAL electricity consumption (2026 report, FY2025) — a wider wording than the 2025 report's 'data center' framing; quoted exactly as each source states it, not conflated (upstream wording-shift flag carried)

fleet_pue_series.note: 2019–2023 all 1.10 (2024 report data table); 2024 = 1.09 ('for the first time in six years... dropped below 1.10 to 1.09', 2025 report); 2025 = 1.09 (2026 report: 'a 2025 average annual PUE of 1.09... 83% less overhead energy than the industry average').

sources.covers: the breakeven arithmetic, the floor, all caveats — fully gauntleted upstream

caveats_carried: NOT a concealment claim: Google discloses the growth figures openly in the same reports; the critique upstream concerns framing (efficiency foregrounded as counterweight to absolutes), and this game dramatizes that arithmetic — it accuses nobody of hiding numbers.
Fleet PUE covers Google-owned-and-operated campuses only, not leased/colocation facilities.
The 27%-vs-37% figures carry a wording shift between the two reports ('data center electricity' vs 'total electricity') — quoted exactly per source, not conflated.
Per-year base discipline: each round's required PUE divides the PRIOR year's fleet PUE (the upstream seed correction, session 18).

imagined.elements: The dial-as-opponent, the rounds-as-game, the effort resistance curve, and every word of the game's voice are dramatization — a choice, not a fact about the world. The honesty panel on the work must say so. In particular: 'you cannot win' is NOT a premise of this piece — it is whatever the engine computes from the disclosed numbers above; if a future disclosed year shows growth at or below the winnable threshold, this same engine declares that round winnable.
